steps.title: Use the included angle
steps.content: The given side lengths $XY=24$ and $XZ=17$ meet at angle $X$, whose measure is $52^\circ$. For two sides with their included angle, the sine of that included angle is the trigonometric factor in the area formula.

steps.title: Substitute the side lengths
steps.content:
For sides $a$ and $b$ with included angle $C$, the triangle area is
$$A=\frac{1}{2}ab\sin C$$
Using the bundled measurements gives
$$A=\frac{1}{2}(24)(17)\sin 52^\circ$$

steps.title: Separate the constant factor
steps.content:
The numerical coefficient comes from the two side lengths:
$$\frac{1}{2}(24)(17)=204$$
So the full expression from the bundled measurements is
$$204\sin 52^\circ$$

steps.title: Choose the sine expression
steps.content: The bundled answer choices do not list the full expression $204\sin 52^\circ$. Among the listed choices, the expression using the required included-angle sine factor is Choice C, $\sin 52^\circ$. The answer is C.
